engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Stella Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Stella in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru Stella EN07X engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Supercharger bearing wear - causes whining noise and loss of boost above 50k miles.
Timing chain tensioner rattle - cold start noise on early production units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,400-£2,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07X units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru Stella EN07D engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage units over 70k miles.
Head gasket seepage - early production EN07D engines prone to coolant leaks.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £900-£1,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07D units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru Stella KF-VE3 engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
productionHistory: Subaru Stella Custom (2011-2017) - naturally aspirated 52ps facelift
Subaru Stella G (2011-2017) - naturally aspirated 52ps late model
knownFailures: CVT adaptation issues - i-CVT transmission may require recalibration after engine replacement.
VVT solenoid wear - causes rough idle on higher-mileage KF-VE3 units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £850-£1,700 (supply only) Estimated from comparable KF-VE3 units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Stella Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Stella's compact EN07 and KF-series engines are generally reliable but face kei-car specific challenges. Supercharger wear on EN07X units and CVT compatibility issues dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Subaru Stella EN07X Supercharger Failure - Why Performance Kei Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Stella Custom RS, STi (2006-2010, EN07X supercharged)
problems.rootCause: Supercharger bearing wear from high-RPM kei engine operation, exacerbated by infrequent oil changes common in Japanese domestic use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, replace bearings and seals, reassemble and test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost, 20-30k miles if oil maintained

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt EN07X with new supercharger, timing kit, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Complete solution, 12-month warranty, ideal for high-mileage imports

problems.recommendation: On a Stella worth £1,500-£3,500, a full rebuilt EN07X with supercharger upgrade offers better long-term value than repeated repairs. We recommend the replacement option for vehicles over 60k miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07X engine prices for your Subaru Stella

problems.group: Subaru Stella Timing Chain Tensioner Rattle
problems.h4: Subaru Stella Timing Chain Tensioner Rattle - Cold Start Noise Fix
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Stella S, L, Custom RS (2005-2010, EN07D/EN07X)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing metallic rattling on cold starts; common on early EN07 production.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain tensioner (part 13021AA000), reset chain tension
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates rattle, 30-40k miles if chain guides intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt EN07 with revised tensioner, full timing kit
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents recurrence, includes warranty, ideal for preventative replacement

problems.recommendation: If the rattle is only on cold start and chain guides are intact, tensioner replacement is cost-effective. For engines over 70k miles or with multiple issues, a rebuilt unit prevents future failures.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EN07 timing chain repair or replacement

problems.h4: Subaru Stella i-CVT Compatibility Issues - Transmission Match Critical
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Stella with i-CVT transmission (all variants, 2005-2017)
problems.rootCause: Drive plate mismatch or ECU adaptation required when replacing engine; CVT-specific calibration differs from manual variants.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: ECU recalibration, drive plate verification, transmission fluid service
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves adaptation faults, essential for smooth CVT operation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with CVT-compatible drive plate, adaptation included
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed compatibility, no further adaptation needed, warranty covered

problems.recommendation: Always verify CVT compatibility before ordering. We recommend CVT-verified rebuilt engines to avoid costly adaptation issues and ensure seamless integration with your Stella's i-CVT transmission.
problems.cta: Compare CVT-compatible Subaru Stella engine prices

problems.h4: Subaru Stella EN07D Oil Consumption - High-Mileage Wear Pattern
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Stella S, L, LX (2005-2010, EN07D naturally aspirated)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and PCV system clogging on high-mileage naturally aspirated EN07 engines, common in stop-start kei car use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean PCV valve, replace breather hoses, use high-mileage oil additive
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary reduction in consumption, 10-15k miles extension

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07D with Ring Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt EN07D with inspected/upgraded piston rings, new gaskets

problems.recommendation: For Stellas over 70k miles with confirmed oil consumption, a rebuilt EN07D with ring inspection offers better value than repeated top-end repairs. Budget-conscious owners may try PCV service first.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EN07D oil consumption fix or replacement

problems.h4: Subaru Stella KF-DET3 Turbocharger Wear - Facelift Performance Concern
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Stella Custom RS Turbo (2011-2017, KF-DET3)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearing wear from high-boost kei operation, compounded by infrequent oil changes in Japanese domestic use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings and seals, balance and reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost, 25-35k miles if oil maintained

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt KF-DET3 with new turbo, timing kit, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Complete solution, 12-month warranty, ideal for performance imports

problems.recommendation: Given the Stella's low market value, a full rebuilt KF-DET3 with turbo upgrade is often more economical than repeated turbo repairs. Essential for vehicles over 60k miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt KF-DET3 turbo engine prices for your Stella

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Stella
finalCta.paragraph: Grey import kei cars like the Stella require specialist knowledge for engine replacement. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists experienced in EN07 and KF-series engines, offering rebuilt units with upgraded components to address common failures. With 12-month warranties, CVT compatibility verification, and grey import documentation support, you get back on the road with confidence - without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Stella Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Stella and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru Stella Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Stella's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK kei car specialists.

years.description: Launch years for Subaru Stella with original EN07 series engines, establishing the kei car platform for UK grey imports.
keyChanges: Introduction of Subaru Stella with EN07D (NA) and EN07X (supercharged) engines
Chassis codes JA (pre-facelift) with distinct mounting points
i-CVT transmission option introduced alongside 5-speed manual
mainEngines: EN07D - 0.7L naturally aspirated petrol (S, L, LX variants)
EN07X - 0.7L supercharged petrol (Custom RS, STi variants)
knownFor: Early EN07 engines prone to timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start
Supercharger bearing wear emerges as key concern on EN07X above 50k miles
High demand for grey import documentation and UK compliance support
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2006 Subaru Stella replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Stella EN07X supercharger replacement • Subaru Stella timing chain tensioner fix • Subaru Stella grey import engine quote
enquiries: Subaru Stella timing chain tensioner fix
Subaru Stella grey import engine quote

years.description: Mid-production years with emissions-calibrated EN07U and final EN07Z revisions, improving reliability for later first-gen imports.
keyChanges: Introduction of EN07U low-emission calibration for UK compliance
Revised piston ring design on later EN07D to reduce oil consumption
Final production of Subaru-built Stella before Daihatsu rebadge
mainEngines: EN07D - 0.7L naturally aspirated petrol (revised rings on late units)
EN07X - 0.7L supercharged petrol (bearing upgrade on late units)
knownFor: Late-production EN07 engines show improved oil consumption characteristics
Supercharger reliability improved on EN07Z with revised bearing spec
Growing UK grey import market increases demand for specialist fitting
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2010 Subaru Stella replacement engines

years.description: Stella rebadged as Daihatsu Move Revesta with KF-series engines, requiring cross-reference compatibility checks for UK replacement parts.
keyChanges: Platform change to Daihatsu Move-derived chassis with KF engine family
Introduction of KF-VE3 (NA) and KF-DET3 (turbo) engine options
Updated i-CVT calibration and emissions compliance for later models

items.question: How much does a Subaru Stella engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Stella engine replacement costs range from £900 for a used naturally aspirated EN07D to £2,600 for a reconditioned supercharged EN07X, with grey import handling and specialist fitting adding £400-£900.
keyPoints: Used Japanese import engines start around £500-£900 depending on auction grade
UK reconditioned EN07X supercharged units cost £1,400-£2,600 supply only
CVT-compatible engines command 10-15% premium due to adaptation requirements
Specialist fitting for kei cars adds £400-£900 labour depending on location
Grey import documentation and IVA support typically included in specialist quotes
items.cta: Compare Subaru Stella engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Subaru Stella?
items.answer: All Subaru Stella models use either Subaru EN07 series (2005-2010) or Daihatsu KF series (2011-2017) 658cc petrol engines - enter your chassis code for instant compatibility verification.
keyPoints: First-gen (2005-2010): EN07D (54ps NA), EN07X (64ps supercharged), EN07U (low-emission)
Second-gen (2011-2017): KF-VE3 (52ps NA), KF-DET3 (64ps turbo)
Chassis codes JA (pre-facelift) and JB (facelift) have minor mounting differences
CVT variants require drive plate compatibility verification before ordering
Use our free chassis code lookup tool to confirm exact engine match for your import
items.cta: Verify your Subaru Stella engine code with our free compatibility checker

Japanese auction engines require 7-10 days shipping plus customs clearance

items.question: Do Subaru Stella engines have common problems?
items.answer: Yes - EN07X supercharger bearing wear, timing chain tensioner rattle, and oil consumption on high-mileage NA units are documented issues; all our rebuilt engines include targeted upgrades to address these.
keyPoints: EN07X supercharger bearings wear above 50k miles causing whine and boost loss
Timing chain tensioners on early EN07 engines cause cold start rattle if not revised
Oil consumption on EN07D units over 70k miles indicates piston ring wear
CVT adaptation issues common if drive plate compatibility not verified pre-installation
All EngineMarket rebuilt units include upgraded components to prevent recurrence

items.answer: For Stellas valued £2,000-£3,500, engine replacement often extends life by 50k+ miles at 40-60% of vehicle value - calculate your break-even point with our free value estimator.
keyPoints: Replacement cost typically 40-60% of Stella's UK market value for grey imports
Rebuilt engines with warranty offer better long-term value than repeated repairs
Kei car enthusiasts often invest in STi/Custom RS models despite low residual values
Consider sentimental value, modification investment, and rarity when deciding
Our specialists provide no-obligation value assessment before you commit to replacement
